def: The marking of a person or group as shameful or unacceptable.
The use of a person or cause merely as a means to an end.
The treating of a whole group as if all its members were alike.
The playing-down of something by setting it against other factors.
A dull, overused remark offered as if it were meaningful.
A secret code; also a symbol standing for something hidden.
A story whose characters and events stand for deeper meanings.
Deliberate exaggeration used for effect, not meant literally.
The quality of being open to more than one interpretation.
Fine, delicate distinction that is not obvious.
Great force and intensity of feeling or expression.
Extreme care and precision in doing something.
Tight logical consistency and exactness.
The placing of something within its wider context to explain it.
The drawing of fine distinctions between things.
A tightening of focus onto a single point; a close overlapping of themes.
To state formally that something is the case.
To lead someone to believe something indirectly.
To imply a further meaning beyond the literal one.
To include something within a larger category or principle.
To foresee and act in advance of something.
To set something within its context to make sense of it.
To work against and frustrate a plan or effect.
To hide a flaw or fact so it is not noticed.
To deny or reject something as untrue or invalid.
To move or waver back and forth between positions.
To rise above or go beyond ordinary limits.
To cause something to continue indefinitely.
To reach the highest or decisive point.
To grow more intense or serious.
To become independent of its origin and beyond control.
To begin to become unsteady or lose certainty.
To become visible or known.
To become established and start to spread.
To grow excessive and hard to control.
Peculiar to one individual; distinctively personal.
Present or found everywhere.
Existing within something as a natural part of it.
Belonging to something as a permanent, essential quality.
Having mixed or contradictory feelings about something.
Firm and clearly expressed; resolute.
Strikingly obvious, especially of something bad.
Surprisingly striking; remarkable.
Dignified and grave in a stately way.
Brief and to the point, often bluntly so.
Marked by fine, subtle distinctions.
Occurring again and again.
Seeking to undermine an established system or authority.
Tending to lead the mind toward a particular idea.
Biased in favour of a particular point of view.
Willing to reconcile; accommodating and agreeable.
Fitting together in a clear, logical, consistent way.
Completely opposed; as far apart as possible.
Serving as a model or typical example.
Stated absolutely, without any qualification.
Seemingly self-contradictory yet perhaps true.
Deep and far-reaching in thought or knowledge.
Basic and undeveloped; at an early stage.
Of minor importance; at the edge.
No longer in use; out of date.
More than is needed; repeated unnecessarily.
Having the opposite effect to the one intended.
Therefore; as a result.
In spite of that; all the same.
Yet; on the other hand; also in the meantime.
For now; until something changes.
In a way that can be seen to progress.
Without being affected by; regardless of.
Through the use of; by way of.
On the strength of; by the authority of.
For the purpose of (archaic, official).
In order to achieve; for the purpose of.
Wholehearted commitment and self-giving to something.
The dead body of a person, especially in formal use.
A dishonourable, contemptible person.
In truth; really (formal or literary).
